claim: Agent performance is increasingly bottlenecked by data quality rather than model capability; high-quality structured data fed to agents matters more than raw model power
actionable: Prioritize data pipeline quality and document structure extraction before scaling to larger models.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Adit Abraham: Better Agents with Better Data

claim: Silent failures in document processing are critical - models misread content without indicating errors, making last-mile verification essential for high-stakes applications
actionable: Implement multi-stage verification loops and confidence scoring when using agents to process documents in healthcare, legal, or financial domains.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Adit Abraham: Better Agents with Better Data

claim: Vision Language Models (VLMs) represent a fundamental shift for document processing; for the first time, models can reliably read handwritten text, handle skewed scans, and manage complex table layouts
actionable: Adopt VLM-based document parsing for PDFs, handwritten forms, and complex documents; combine with traditional CV for layout detection when determinism is required.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Adit Abraham: Better Agents with Better Data

claim: Combine traditional Computer Vision (object detection, table segmentation) with LLMs as correction agents - CV provides determinism and structure, LLMs catch semantic mistakes
actionable: In document extraction pipelines, use traditional CV for layout detection and bounding boxes, then apply speculative decoding with LLMs to fix character-level errors without losing positional information.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Adit Abraham: Better Agents with Better Data

claim: Output formatting significantly impacts downstream model performance; use markdown for simple tables, HTML for complex tables with merged cells, and natural language summaries for embedding/retrieval
actionable: Dynamically select table format (markdown/HTML) based on complexity; generate natural language table summaries specifically for embedding-based retrieval, then use original HTML for context to the LLM.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Adit Abraham: Better Agents with Better Data

claim: Agent harnesses with validation loops enable superhuman performance on structured extraction; models can iteratively improve outputs by checking logical constraints (e.g., invoice line items summing to total)
actionable: Build validation rules into sub-agents within extraction pipelines; use agentic verification to re-process and audit outputs until business logic constraints are satisfied.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Adit Abraham: Better Agents with Better Data

claim: Evaluation at every pipeline stage is non-negotiable; first evaluate parsing/extraction accuracy, then retrieval precision, then formatting impact, then end-to-end system performance
actionable: Create evaluation checkpoints at each pipeline stage; don't just measure end-to-end accuracy—isolate errors to source (parsing, retrieval, formatting, reasoning).
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Adit Abraham: Better Agents with Better Data

claim: Shift from RAG to file-based agent architecture where agents navigate document file systems with tools; this reduces chunking/retrieval friction and allows agents to decide when they need more context
actionable: Instead of embedding and retrieving chunks, give agents file-based access with metadata (bounding boxes, summaries) so they can explore documents and cite sources directly.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Adit Abraham: Better Agents with Better Data
